mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, labor market deterioration signals severe demand destruction and rising recession probabilities, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with a combined Rank IC below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative or flat 5-day forward returns during expansionary regimes, or the mechanism shows zero predictive accuracy in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stable or increasing federal funds rates indicates monetary tightening that erodes real yields and corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% monthly while FEDFUNDS remains flat or increases
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and non-falling FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with a hit rate below 40% in historical regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP contractions exceeding 1.0% signal severe economic contraction and earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances following GDPC1 contractions below -1.0%, invalidating the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment confirms broad-based economic recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during post-recession periods.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, with a combined predictive accuracy below 50% in walk-forward tests.
